data.name: Absol

attacks.name: Allure
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

data.flavorText: Because of this Pokémon's ability to detect danger, people mistook Absol as a bringer of doom.

data.name: Sandile
data.flavorText: It submerges itself in sand and moves as if swimming. This wise behavior keeps its enemies from finding it and maintains its temperature.

data.name: Krokorok
data.flavorText: Protected by thin membranes, their eyes can see even in the dead of night. They live in groups of a few individuals.

data.name: Krookodile

attacks.name: Vengeful Fang
attacks.text: If any of your Pokémon were Knocked Out by damage from an attack during your opponent's last turn, this attack does 160 more damage.

data.flavorText: After clamping down with its powerful jaws, it twists its body around to rip its prey in half.

data.name: Toxel

attacks.name: Call for Family
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 2 Basic Pokémon and put them onto your Bench. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: It has no problem drinking dirty water. An organ inside Toxel's body filters such water into a poisonous liquid that is harmless to Toxel.

data.name: Toxtricity

abilities.name: Sinister Surge
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for a Basic Darkness Energy card and attach it to 1 of your Benched Darkness Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck. If you attached Energy to a Pokémon in this way, place 2 damage counters on that Pokémon.

data.flavorText: As it scatters toxic sweat and emits electricity, a melody that sounds like it came from a guitar reverberates through the surrounding area.

data.name: Eternatus

attacks.name: Shatter
attacks.text: Discard a Stadium in play.

attacks.name: Power Rush
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, during your next turn, this Pokémon can't use attacks.

data.flavorText: The core on its chest absorbs energy emanating from the lands of the Galar region. This energy is what allows Eternatus to stay active.

rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Emperor's Stance

attacks.name: Iron Feathers
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, this Pokémon takes 60 less damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.name: Bronzor

attacks.name: Iron Defense
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, during your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks.

data.flavorText: Ancient people believed that the pattern on Bronzor's back contained a mysterious power.

data.name: Bronzong

attacks.name: Triple Draw
attacks.text: Draw 3 cards.

attacks.name: Tool Drop
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each Pokémon Tool attached to all Pokémon.

data.flavorText: In ages past, this Pokémon was revered as a bringer of rain. It was found buried in the ground.

data.name: Togedemaru

attacks.name: Find a Friend
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: When it's in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

data.name: Duraludon

attacks.name: Hyper Beam
attacks.text: Discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Duraludon's body is comprised of a special metal that's lightweight and scratch resistant. It's also smooth, as though it was given a mirror finish.

data.name: Archaludon

attacks.name: Coated Attack
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, prevent all damage done to this Pokémon by attacks from Basic Pokémon.

data.flavorText: It gathers static electricity from its surroundings. The beams it launches when down on all fours are tremendously powerful.

data.name: Jigglypuff

attacks.name: Ball Roll
attacks.text: Flip a coin until you get tails. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

data.flavorText: When its huge eyes waver, it sings a mysteriously soothing melody that lulls its enemies to sleep.

data.name: Wigglytuff

attacks.name: Round
attacks.text: This attack does 40 damage for each of your Pokémon in play that has the Round attack.

data.flavorText: It has a very fine fur. Take care not to make it angry, or it may inflate steadily and hit with a body slam.

data.name: Aipom

attacks.name: Astonish
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand, and your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

data.flavorText: It lives atop tall trees. When leaping from branch to branch, it deftly uses its tail for balance.

data.name: Ambipom

attacks.name: Dual Tail
attacks.text: Discard 2 Energy from this Pokémon, and this attack does 60 damage to each of 2 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon.)

data.flavorText: They live on large trees. They are said to communicate by connecting their tails to those of others.

data.name: Smeargle

attacks.name: Energizing Sketch
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. Attach an amount of Basic Energy up to the number of heads from your discard pile to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

data.flavorText: Once a Smeargle reaches adulthood, it will have other members of its species leave paw prints on its back.

data.name: Zigzagoon

attacks.name: Surprise Attack
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If tails, this attack does nothing.

data.flavorText: A Pokémon with abundant curiosity. It shows an interest in everything, so it always zigzags.

abilities.name: Excited Dash
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon is on your Bench, and if you have any Mega Evolution Pokémon ex in play, you may use this Ability. Switch this Pokémon with your Active Pokémon.

data.flavorText: It uses its explosive speed and razor-sharp claws to bring down prey. Running along winding paths is not its strong suit.

data.name: Buneary

attacks.name: Run Around
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

data.flavorText: Buneary can attack by rolling up their ears and then striking with the force created by unrolling them. This attack becomes stronger with training.

attacks.name: Play Rough

attacks.name: Energy Straw
attacks.text: Your opponent reveals their hand, and this attack does 80 damage for each Energy card you find there.

data.flavorText: Vibrantly patterned wings are its prominent feature. It sucks sweet flower nectar with its long mouth.

data.name: Dustox

abilities.name: Boisterous Wind
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Flip a coin. If heads, put an Energy attached to your opponent's Active Pokémon into their hand.

attacks.name: Twilight Poison
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Asleep and Poisoned.

data.flavorText: A nocturnal Pokémon. Drawn by streetlights, they messily eat the leaves of trees lining boulevards.

data.name: Croconaw

attacks.name: Crunch
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, discard an Energy from your opponent's Active Pokémon.

data.flavorText: If it loses a fang, a new one grows back in its place. There are always 48 fangs lining its mouth.

data.name: Mega Feraligatr ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Mortal Crunch
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon already has any damage counters on it, this attack does 200 more damage.

data.name: N's Vanillish

attacks.name: Sheer Cold
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't use attacks.

data.flavorText: They cool down the surrounding air and create ice particles, which they use to freeze their foes.

data.name: Raichu

attacks.name: Quick Blow
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 50 more damage.

attacks.name: Strong Volt
attacks.text: Discard a Lightning Energy from this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: When its electricity builds, its muscles are stimulated, and it becomes more aggressive than usual.

data.name: Tynamo

attacks.name: Hold Still
attacks.text: Heal 10 damage from this Pokémon.

data.flavorText: While one alone doesn’t have much power, a chain of many Tynamo can be as powerful as lightning.

data.name: Stunfisk

attacks.name: Pouncing Trap
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat. During your next turn, the Defending Pokémon takes 100 more damage from attacks (after applying Weakness and Resistance).

data.flavorText: It conceals itself in the mud of the seashore. Then it waits. When prey touch it, it delivers a jolt of electricity.

data.name: Helioptile
data.flavorText: The frills on either side of its head have cells that generate electricity when exposed to sunlight.

data.name: Charjabug
data.flavorText: While its durable shell protects it from attacks, Charjabug strikes at enemies with jolts of electricity discharged from the tips of its jaws.

data.name: Vikavolt

attacks.name: Volt Switch
attacks.text: Switch this Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Lightning Pokémon.

data.flavorText: When carrying a Charjabug, Vikavolt can receive electricity from it and then rapidly fire powerful electromagnetic beams from its large jaws.

data.name: Iono's Kilowattrel

abilities.name: Flashing Draw
abilities.text: You must discard a Basic Lightning Energy from this Pokémon in order to use this Ability. Once during your turn, you may draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.

data.flavorText: Kilowattrel inflates its throat sac to amplify its electricity. By riding the wind, this Pokémon can fly over 430 miles in a day.

data.name: Clefairy
data.flavorText: On nights with a full moon, they gather together and dance. The surrounding area is enveloped in an abnormal magnetic field.

data.name: Lillie's Clefairy ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Fairy Zone
abilities.text: The Weakness of each of your opponent's Dragon Pokémon in play is now Psychic. (Apply Weakness as ×2.)

attacks.name: Full Moon Rondo
attacks.text: This attack does 20 more damage for each Benched Pokémon (both yours and your opponent's).

data.name: Team Rocket's Exeggutor

attacks.name: Tri Kinesis
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. If all of them are heads, Knock Out 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Double-Edge
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 30 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: It is said that on rare occasions, one of its heads will drop off and continue on as an Exeggcute.

data.name: Team Rocket's Mewtwo ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Power Saver
abilities.text: This Pokémon can't attack unless you have 4 or more Team Rocket's Pokémon in play.

attacks.name: Erasure Ball
attacks.text: You may discard up to 2 Energy from your Benched Pokémon. This attack does 60 more damage for each card you discarded in this way.

data.name: Misdreavus

attacks.name: Ascension
attacks.text: Search your deck for a card that evolves from this Pokémon and put it onto this Pokémon to evolve it. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: It loves to bite and yank people's hair from behind without warning, just to see their shocked reactions.

data.name: Ralts

attacks.name: Collect
attacks.text: Draw a card.

data.flavorText: The horns on its head provide a strong power that enables it to sense people’s emotions.

data.name: Mega Gardevoir ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Overflowing Wishes
attacks.text: For each of your Benched Pokémon, search your deck for a Basic Psychic Energy card and attach it to that Pokémon. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Mega Symphonia
attacks.text: This attack does 50 damage for each Psychic Energy attached to all of your Pokémon.

data.name: Banette

attacks.name: Cursed Words
attacks.text: Your opponent chooses 3 cards from their hand and shuffles those cards into their deck.

data.flavorText: This Pokémon developed from an abandoned doll that amassed a grudge. It is seen in dark alleys.

data.name: Team Rocket's Mimikyu

attacks.name: Gemstone Mimicry
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Tera Pokémon's attacks and use it as this attack.

data.flavorText: This Pokémon lives in dark places untouched by sunlight. When it appears before humans, it hides itself under a cloth that resembles a Pikachu.

data.name: Munkidori

abilities.name: Adrena-Brain
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if this Pokémon has any Darkness Energy attached, you may move up to 3 damage counters from 1 of your Pokémon to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon.

attacks.name: Mind Bend
attacks.text: Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Confused.

data.flavorText: Munkidori keeps itself somewhere safe while it toys with its foes, using psychokinesis to induce intense dizziness.

data.name: Hitmontop

attacks.name: Spin and Draw
attacks.text: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards.

data.flavorText: It spins on its horn while dealing out elegant kicks. Its horn grows continuously through its lifetime.

attacks.name: Seventh Kick
attacks.text: If you don't have exactly 7 cards in your hand, this attack does nothing.

data.name: Lunatone

abilities.name: Lunar Cycle
abilities.text: Once during your turn, if you have Solrock in play, you may discard a Basic Fighting Energy card from your hand in order to use this Ability. Draw 3 cards. You can't use more than 1 Lunar Cycle Ability each turn.

data.flavorText: It was discovered at the site of a meteor strike 40 years ago. Its stare can lull its foes to sleep.

data.name: Regirock ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Regi Charge
attacks.text: Attach up to 2 Basic Fighting Energy cards from your discard pile to this Pokémon.

attacks.name: Giant Rock
attacks.text: If your opponent's Active Pokémon is a Stage 2 Pokémon, this attack does 140 more damage.

data.name: Stunfisk ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Big Bite
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, the Defending Pokémon can't retreat.

attacks.name: Flopping Trap
attacks.text: If this Pokémon has any damage counters on it, this attack does 100 more damage.

data.name: Mega Hawlucha ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.
Mega Evolution ex Rule: When your Mega Evolution Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 3 Prize cards.

abilities.name: Tenacious Body
abilities.text: If this Pokémon would be Knocked Out by damage from an attack, flip a coin. If heads, this Pokémon is not Knocked Out, and its remaining HP becomes 10.

attacks.name: Somersault Dive
attacks.text: If a Stadium is in play, this attack does 140 more damage. Then, discard that Stadium.

data.name: Haunter
data.flavorText: It likes to lurk in the dark and tap shoulders with a gaseous hand. Its touch causes endless shuddering.

data.name: Team Rocket's Murkrow

attacks.name: Deceit
attacks.text: Search your deck for a Supporter card, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Torment
attacks.text: Choose 1 of your opponent's Active Pokémon's attacks. During your opponent's next turn, that Pokémon can't use that attack.

data.flavorText: Feared and loathed by many, it is believed to bring misfortune to all those who see it at night.

data.name: Team Rocket's Honchkrow

attacks.name: Rocket Feathers
attacks.text: You may discard any number of Supporter cards that have "Team Rocket" in their name from your hand, and this attack does 60 damage for each card you discarded in this way.

data.flavorText: A single cry from this nocturnal Pokémon, and more than 100 of its Murkrow cronies will assemble.

data.name: Galarian Zigzagoon
data.flavorText: Its restlessness has it constantly running around. If it sees another Pokémon, it will purposely run into them in order to start a fight.

data.name: Scraggy

attacks.name: Knock Off
attacks.text: Discard a random card from your opponent's hand.

data.flavorText: Its skin has a rubbery elasticity, so it can reduce damage by defensively pulling its skin up to its neck.

data.name: Pawniard

attacks.name: Push Down
attacks.text: Switch out your opponent's Active Pokémon to the Bench. (Your opponent chooses the new Active Pokémon.)

data.flavorText: Any chips in its blades would prove fatal for it. After each battle, it diligently maintains its blades using its favorite sharpening stone.

data.name: Bisharp

attacks.name: Rapid Draw
attacks.text: Draw 2 cards.

data.flavorText: Bisharp mercilessly cuts its opponents to pieces with the sharp blades covering its body. It will do anything to win.

data.name: Kingambit

abilities.name: Supreme Overlord
abilities.text: Attacks used by this Pokémon do 30 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon for each Prize card your opponent has taken (before applying Weakness and Resistance).

attacks.name: Double-Edged Slash
attacks.text: This Pokémon also does 50 damage to itself.

data.flavorText: Though it commands a massive army in battle, it’s not skilled at devising complex strategies. It just uses brute strength to keep pushing.

data.name: Togedemaru ex
rules: Pokémon ex rule: When your Pokémon ex is Knocked Out, your opponent takes 2 Prize cards.

attacks.name: Stun Needle
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

attacks.name: Spiky Rolling
attacks.text: If this Pokémon used Spiky Rolling during your last turn, this attack does 80 more damage.

data.name: Team Rocket's Meowth

attacks.name: Paw-cket Pilfer
attacks.text: Choose a random card from your opponent's hand. Your opponent reveals that card and shuffles it into their deck.

attacks.name: Fury Swipes
attacks.text: Flip 3 coins. This attack does 20 damage for each heads.

data.flavorText: All it does is sleep during the daytime. At night, it patrols its territory with its eyes aglow.

data.name: Drampa

attacks.name: Dragon Strike
attacks.text: During your next turn, this Pokémon can't use Dragon Strike.

data.flavorText: It has a compassionate personality, but if it is angered, it completely destroys its surroundings with its intense breath.

data.name: Terapagos

attacks.name: Prism Charge
attacks.text: Search your deck for up to 3 Basic Energy cards of different types and attach them to your Tera Pokémon in any way you like. Then, shuffle your deck.

data.flavorText: The shell is made of crystallized Terastal energy. When struck by a move, this shell absorbs the move’s energy and transfers it to Terapagos.

data.name: Air Balloon
rules: The Retreat Cost of the Pokémon this card is attached to is ColorlessColorless less.
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

data.name: Boss's Orders
rules: Switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Fighting Gong
rules: Search your deck for a Basic Fighting Energy card or a Basic Fighting Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Iris's Fighting Spirit
rules: You can use this card only if you discard another card from your hand.  Draw cards until you have 6 cards in your hand.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Light Ball
rules: Attacks used by the Pikachu ex this card is attached to do 50 more damage to your opponent's Active Pokémon ex (before applying Weakness and Resistance).
You may attach any number of Pokémon Tools to your Pokémon during your turn. You may attach only 1 Pokémon Tool to each Pokémon, and it stays attached.

data.name: Lillie's Determination
rules: Shuffle your hand into your deck. Then, draw 6 cards. If you have exactly 6 Prize cards remaining, draw 8 cards instead.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Mystery Garden
rules: Once during each player's turn, that player may discard an Energy card from their hand in order to draw cards until they have as many cards in their hand as they have Psychic Pokémon in play.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

data.name: Nighttime Mine
rules: Attacks used by each Tera Pokémon in play (both yours and your opponent's) cost Colorless more.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

data.name: Poké Pad
rules: Search your deck for a Pokémon that doesn't have a Rule Box, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck. (Pokémon ex, Pokémon V, etc. have Rule Boxes.)
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Team Rocket's Archer
rules: You can use this card only if any of your Team Rocket's Pokémon were Knocked Out during your opponent's last turn.  Each player shuffles their hand into their deck. Then, you draw 5 cards, and your opponent draws 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Team Rocket's Factory
rules: Once during each player's turn, if they played a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name from their hand this turn, they may draw 2 cards.
You may play only 1 Stadium card during your turn. Put it next to the Active Spot, and discard it if another Stadium comes into play. A Stadium with the same name can’t be played.

data.name: Team Rocket's Giovanni
rules: Switch your Active Team Rocket's Pokémon with 1 of your Benched Team Rocket's Pokémon. If you do, switch in 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon to the Active Spot.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Team Rocket's Transceiver
rules: Search your deck for a Supporter card that has "Team Rocket" in its name, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.
You may play any number of Item cards during your turn.

data.name: Urbain
rules: Draw 3 cards.
You may play only 1 Supporter card during your turn.

data.name: Erika's Tangela

abilities.name: Gathering of Blossoms
abilities.text: Once during your turn, you may use this Ability. Search your deck for an Erika's Pokémon, reveal it, and put it into your hand. Then, shuffle your deck.

attacks.name: Bind
attacks.text: Flip a coin. If heads, your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: Hidden beneath a tangle of vines that grows nonstop even if the vines are torn off, this Pokémon’s true appearance remains a mystery.

data.name: Budew

attacks.name: Itchy Pollen
attacks.text: During your opponent's next turn, they can't play any Item cards from their hand.

data.flavorText: The pollen it releases contains poison. If this Pokémon is raised on clean water, the poison’s toxicity is increased.

data.name: Raboot
data.flavorText: While it prides itself on its varied kicking moves, it can also deliver powerful headbutts once its flames have heated up its forehead.

data.name: Weavile

attacks.name: Hail Claw
attacks.text: Discard all Energy from this Pokémon. Your opponent's Active Pokémon is now Paralyzed.

data.flavorText: They travel in groups of four or five, leaving signs for one another on trees and rocks. They bring down their prey with coordinated attacks.
